seo_title: Current Time in Dakar — Greenwich Mean Time (GMT, UTC+0)
seo_description: Current time in Dakar, Senegal. Live GMT clock — UTC+0 year-round, no Daylight Saving Time. Dakar time vs London, Paris, Abidjan, New York.

faq.question: What time is it in Dakar right now?
faq.answer: Dakar operates on Greenwich Mean Time (GMT/UTC+0) year-round with no Daylight Saving Time adjustments. As Senegal's capital and largest city, Dakar maintains the same UTC+0 offset in every season. To see the exact current time in Dakar with live seconds, use our real-time clock above.

faq.question: What time zone is Dakar in?
faq.answer: Dakar is in the Greenwich Mean Time zone (GMT/UTC+0). Senegal does not observe Daylight Saving Time, so Dakar stays at UTC+0 throughout the entire year. This makes Dakar one of the few major capitals that shares its offset with London during winter months (when London is also on GMT), though London shifts to BST (UTC+1) in summer while Dakar remains at UTC+0.

faq.question: Does Dakar observe Daylight Saving Time?
faq.answer: No. Dakar and all of Senegal do not observe Daylight Saving Time. The clocks in Dakar never change — they remain at UTC+0 (GMT) year-round. This makes scheduling calls and meetings with Dakar straightforward, as the offset from other cities only changes when those other cities adjust their own clocks for DST.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Dakar and London?
faq.answer: During winter (October–March), Dakar and London are at the same time — both on GMT/UTC+0. From late March to late October, London moves to British Summer Time (BST/UTC+1), making London 1 hour ahead of Dakar. When it is 3:00 PM in London during summer, it is 2:00 PM in Dakar.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Dakar and New York?
faq.answer: Dakar is 5 hours ahead of New York (EST/UTC-5) during winter, and 4 hours ahead during US Daylight Saving Time (EDT/UTC-4). When it is 9:00 AM in New York (EST), it is 2:00 PM in Dakar. For business calls, the best overlap window is 9:00 AM–1:00 PM New York time (2:00 PM–6:00 PM Dakar time).

faq.question: What is the time difference between Dakar and Paris?
faq.answer: Dakar is 1 hour behind Paris during winter (CET/UTC+1) and 2 hours behind Paris during European summer (CEST/UTC+2). When it is 12:00 PM in Paris in summer, it is 10:00 AM in Dakar. France's colonial ties with Senegal make Paris–Dakar the most active international business corridor for the city.

faq.question: What is the best time to call Dakar from Europe?
faq.answer: From the UK (winter, GMT), you can call Dakar any time during business hours since both are on UTC+0. From France or Germany (CET), call between 10:00 AM–6:00 PM Paris time to reach Dakar between 9:00 AM–5:00 PM local time. During European summer (CEST), call between 11:00 AM–7:00 PM Paris time for the same Dakar window.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Dakar?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Dakar run 8:00 AM–5:00 PM GMT (UTC+0) Monday through Friday, with a midday break common in many offices from 1:00 PM–3:00 PM. Government offices typically operate 8:00 AM–4:00 PM. The Friday prayer (Jumaa) affects schedules from around 1:00 PM–2:30 PM. Banks generally open 8:00 AM–4:30 PM Monday–Thursday and 8:00 AM–12:30 PM on Fridays.

content_blocks.title: Dakar Time Zone — UTC+0 Year-Round
content_blocks.content: Dakar, the capital of Senegal, operates on Greenwich Mean Time (GMT/UTC+0) permanently. Unlike most European capitals, Dakar never adjusts its clocks for Daylight Saving Time, making it one of Africa's most predictable time zones for international scheduling. Situated at the Cape Verde Peninsula — the westernmost point of mainland Africa — Dakar sits geographically at the same longitude as the Canary Islands and Portugal. In winter months, Dakar shares the same time as London, Lisbon, Reykjavik, and Abidjan. In summer, when Europe advances its clocks, Dakar becomes 1–2 hours behind its main European trading partners. The city's UTC+0 permanence simplifies business scheduling for multinational firms operating across West Africa, as no seasonal clock change disrupts meeting patterns.

content_blocks.title: Dakar as a West African Business and Fintech Hub
content_blocks.content: Dakar has emerged as one of West Africa's premier business gateways, hosting regional headquarters for major banks, telecoms, and development organizations. French is the official language, making Dakar the natural commercial bridge between francophone West Africa and Europe — particularly France, Belgium, and Switzerland. The city's fintech sector has grown rapidly, with startups such as Wave and InTouch reshaping mobile money across the region. Dakar is also home to BCEAO (the West African Central Bank), making it the financial capital of the CFA franc zone. Key business hours align closely with Paris CET despite Dakar's UTC+0 position, so European mornings overlap well with Dakar afternoons. The Blaise Diagne International Airport connects Dakar directly to Paris, Brussels, New York, and Istanbul, supporting a growing volume of international trade and investment.

content_blocks.title: Calling and Scheduling with Dakar Internationally
content_blocks.content: Dakar's stable UTC+0 offset makes international call scheduling relatively simple. From New York (EST/UTC-5), the optimal window to reach Dakar during business hours is 4:00 AM–12:00 PM Eastern — or more practically, morning calls before 1:00 PM EST catch Dakar in mid-to-late afternoon. From London in winter (also UTC+0), there is no offset at all. From Paris or Berlin (CET/UTC+1), Dakar is 1 hour behind in winter and 2 hours behind in summer when Europe switches to CEST. From Dubai (GST/UTC+4), Dakar is 4 hours behind year-round. Businesses in Dubai calling Dakar should aim for 1:00 PM–6:00 PM Dubai time to catch Dakar's 9:00 AM–2:00 PM window. Dakar's dialing code is +221, and major telecom providers include Orange Senegal, Expresso, and Free Senegal.
